definitions: To leave a meeting, performance, or other event, especially as an expression of protest or disapproval.
To go on strike; to stop working as a form of protest.
To abandon someone or something suddenly, especially a partner or a job.
examples: The audience began to walk out during the boring speech.
The workers threatened to walk out if their demands were not met.
She decided to walk out on her husband after years of unhappiness.
